name: Cassis Jelly
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Fresh currant juice (or equal parts fresh cranberry and apple juice)
or commercially canned unprocessed juice, strained
recipeInstructions:
Makes 4 - 1/2 pint jars
Notes: A perfect garnish for poultry and game. A gourmet replacement in all desserts, sauces, or glazes calling for currant jelly.
1. Place fruit juice, cassis, lemon juice, and sugar in a heavy saucepan over high heat.
2. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
3. Add liquid pectin and continue boiling until the mixture reaches a full, rolling boil.
4. Boil for 1 minute after reaching a full rolling boil.
5. Remove from heat and skim off foam using a metal spoon.
6. Pour into hot sterilized jars and seal with lids (using the hot water bath method). The jelly can also be refrigerated up to 6 weeks.
